task_completion.description: Verify that the measurement is performed on the axial slice of the MRI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The measurement line is visible in the axial viewport (typically the top-right or top-left view, often outlined in red or explicitly labeled 'Axial' in medical imaging software).

task_completion.description: Verify that a measurement line (markup) has been created on the tumor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A distinct line with endpoints (control points) is drawn over the hyperintense (bright) tumor region in the MRI.

task_completion.description: Verify that the measurement line spans the maximum diameter of the tumor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The drawn line visually crosses the widest part of the tumor on the displayed slice, extending from one outer edge of the tumor to the opposite outer edge.

task_completion.description: Verify that the numeric measurement value is recorded and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A numeric value representing the length (usually in millimeters, 'mm') is displayed directly next to the measurement line on the image, or is clearly visible in a markups/measurement table in the software GUI.

task_completion.description: Verify that a screenshot showing the measurement is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog box, screen capture module interface, or terminal command explicitly showing the file being saved to '~/Documents/SlicerData/Screenshots/tumor_measurement.png'.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the medical imaging software GUI to perform the measurement.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the interface of a medical imaging tool (like 3D Slicer) with active viewports, rather than just a script or text editor.

integrity.description: Verify that the measurement is a genuine software markup and not artificially overlaid text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The measurement line and text should match the native UI styling of the imaging software's markup tools, not look like it was pasted in an image editor.

integrity.description: Verify that the screenshot was saved exactly to the requested path without shortcuts.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file path '~/Documents/SlicerData/Screenshots/tumor_measurement.png' should be visible in the save dialog or command, without evidence of the agent renaming a pre-existing file to fake completion.
